title: Wah Kaba Falls
tagline: Wah Kaba Falls — heritage city of Meghalaya

overview.short: Wah Kaba Falls is a waterfall located near Cherrapunji in Meghalaya, in the north eastern India.
overview.description: Wah Kaba Falls is a waterfall located near Cherrapunji in Meghalaya, in the north eastern India. The waterfall descends from a steep rockface and drops approximately 170–190 metres into a gorge. There is a local legend that two fairies live at the falls. The falls can be seen on the way to Cherrapunji from Shillong.

topPlaces.name: Cherrapunji
topPlaces.description: Cherrapunji ( ), also known as Sohra, is a sub-divisional town (Proposed District) in the East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It was the traditional capital of ka hima Sohra (Khasi tribal kingdom).

topPlaces.name: Wei Sawdong Falls
topPlaces.description: Wei Sawdong Falls is a three-tiered waterfall located in Cherrapunji, Meghalaya.

topPlaces.name: Nohkalikai Falls
topPlaces.description: Nohkalikai Falls, sometimes Ka Likai, is the tallest plunge waterfall in India. The 340 metres (1,115 ft) waterfall is located in the Indian state of Meghalaya, near Cherapunji (now known as Sohra), one of the wettest places on Earth.

topPlaces.name: Nohsngithiang Falls
topPlaces.description: Nohsngithiang Falls (also known as the Seven Sisters Waterfalls or Mawsmai Falls) is a seven-segmented waterfall located 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) south of Mawsmai village in East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. The water falls from a height of 315 metres (1,033 ft) and has an average width of 70 metres (230 ft).

topPlaces.name: Umshiang Double-Decker Root Bridge
topPlaces.description: Umshiang Double-Decker Root Bridge is a living root bridge in Meghalaya, India. It can be reached via trekking 45 kilometres (28 mi), or 6500 steps, from Cherrapunji.

topPlaces.name: Kynrem Falls
topPlaces.description: The Kynrem Falls is located 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from Cherrapunji in East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It is situated inside the Thangkharang Park.

items.note: Cherrapunji ( ), also known as Sohra, is a sub-divisional town (Proposed District) in the East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It was the traditional capital of ka hima Sohra (Khasi tribal kingdom).
Wei Sawdong Falls is a three-tiered waterfall located in Cherrapunji, Meghalaya.

Nohkalikai Falls, sometimes Ka Likai, is the tallest plunge waterfall in India. The 340 metres (1,115 ft) waterfall is located in the Indian state of Meghalaya, near Cherapunji (now known as Sohra), one of the wettest places on Earth.

Nohsngithiang Falls (also known as the Seven Sisters Waterfalls or Mawsmai Falls) is a seven-segmented waterfall located 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) south of Mawsmai village in East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. The water falls from a height of 315 metres (1,033 ft) and has an average width of 70 metres (230 ft).
Umshiang Double-Decker Root Bridge is a living root bridge in Meghalaya, India. It can be reached via trekking 45 kilometres (28 mi), or 6500 steps, from Cherrapunji.
The Kynrem Falls is located 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from Cherrapunji in East Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It is situated inside the Thangkharang Park.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Wah Kaba Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Wah Kaba Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Wah Kaba Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shillong Airport (Umroi) (50 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (97 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Wah Kaba Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Wah Kaba Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Wah Kaba Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Wah Kaba Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Wah Kaba Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Cherrapunji, Wei Sawdong Falls, Nohkalikai Falls, Nohsngithiang Falls, Umshiang Double-Decker Root Bridge.
